name: Reticulum
transport: Any (LoRa, TCP, packet radio, serial, I2P)
summary: Cryptographic networking stack that runs over any transport. Self-configuring, addresses are public keys, forward-secret by default.
why: An internet that doesn't depend on global IP, DNS, or CAs — every destination is a key, every hop is encrypted.
tradeoffs: Smaller ecosystem than IP. Higher per-packet overhead than raw LoRa. No central directory — discovery is gossip-based.
